attacking_style: Targets steepest gradient sections of climbs to maximize physiological advantage, typically launching decisive moves on final categorized ascents. Demonstrates precision timing rather than sustained long-range attacks.
team_tactics: Sky deploys dual-protection strategy, placing him in breaks while maintaining peloton control. Receives strong defensive support when in race lead, with team controlling tempo even without numerical superiority on final climbs.
terrain_preference: Steep gradient climbs where power-to-weight ratio is decisive. Thrives in stage races with multiple categorized climbs in finale. Capable in uphill time trials based on positioning strategy.
vulnerability: Requires team defensive structure when protecting slim GC margins (2-second defense needed desperate chase). Limited evidence of ability to win without Sky's controlling infrastructure.
key_patterns: Attacks on steepest gradient sections specifically, not summit finishes (3 observations)
Sky uses him in dual-GC tactics, placing in breaks while controlling peloton (2 observations)
Positions tactically for bonus seconds and sprint finishes to defend GC leads (2 observations)
Defends race leads under maximum pressure with minimal domestique support successfully (2 observations)
Targeted by rivals as closer GC threat warranting immediate response over distant contenders (2 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: 2019 observation shows attacking but failing to make winning move, contrasting with 2017 dominance and race victory at same event
